# `sesam hook post-checkout` is run by git after a checkout (and after clone).
# It must reveal the secrets the caller can access into the worktree so the
# freshly checked-out sealed objects show up as usable plaintext.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

# Init, register a secret and commit the sealed object.
exec sesam init --user admin
cp secret.env app.env
exec sesam add app.env --group admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam with a secret'

# Simulate a fresh checkout: the gitignored plaintext is absent, only the
# sealed object is present.
rm app.env
! exists app.env

# The hook reveals the secret back into the worktree.
exec sesam hook post-checkout
exists app.env
grep 'super-secret' app.env

# On a repo that was never initialized, the hook is a silent no-op.
cd $WORK
mkdir plain
cd plain
exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'
exec sesam hook post-checkout
! stdout .

-- secret.env --
TOKEN=super-secret
